name: salary-review
description: Prepare for a raise conversation. Research market rates, build your comparison matrix, plan the negotiation, and draft the ask. Walk in with data, not feelings.

config.instruction: Research market salary data for the user's role, location, experience level, and industry. Sources: Glassdoor ranges, Levels.fyi (for tech), BLS data, industry salary surveys. Produce: market median, 25th/75th percentile, and how the user's current salary compares.

Build the value case: compare user's contributions against their compensation. Include: responsibilities vs job description, notable achievements, revenue/cost impact, skills acquired, and any scope creep (doing more than hired for). Quantify everything possible.

Plan the negotiation: target number (what to ask for), walk-away number (minimum acceptable), BATNA (what's your alternative?), anticipated objections and responses, timing (when to ask), and non-salary levers (equity, PTO, remote, title, bonus).

Draft the salary review request: email to schedule the conversation AND talking points for the meeting itself. Professional, data-driven, collaborative tone. Lead with value delivered, then market data, then the ask.
